newsBriefing: Today's top stories span blockbuster NFL moves, standout performances and a major college-sports policy push. The Raiders agreed to send Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for 2026 and 2027 first-round picks, while the Bears also stayed active by agreeing to acquire center Garrett Bradbury from the Patriots for a 2027 fifth-rounder. In college hoops, No. 19 Miami (Ohio) finished the regular season as Division I men's lone unbeaten team at 31-0 after a 110-108 overtime win at Ohio, and Vanderbilt women's coach Shea Ralph was ejected in an 89-78 SEC quarterfinal loss to Ole Miss that likely dented the Commodores' No. 1 seed hopes. In basketball and baseball spotlight games, Jayson Tatum returned from his Achilles injury with 15 points in Boston's 120-100 win over Dallas, Victor Wembanyama led San Antonio's 25-point comeback over the Clippers and called it one of the best wins of his life, and Aaron Judge homered as Team USA opened the World Baseball Classic with a 15-5 rout of Brazil. Off the field, President Donald Trump said he plans to issue an executive order within a week after a White House roundtable on NIL, athlete status and the broader economics of college sports.
